# @hoge1e3/dom-id

Generates hierarchical, unique DOM element IDs for use in reactive component trees.

Each call to `id()` creates a globally unique root ID. Property access on the returned `Idgen` object creates child scopes, enabling components to manage their own namespaced ID subtrees without collision.

## Installation

```sh
npm install @hoge1e3/dom-id
```

## API

### `id(pre?: string): Idgen`

Creates a root `Idgen` with an optional prefix string. The actual ID is made globally unique by appending a counter suffix, ensuring no two calls to `id()` return the same base ID even with the same prefix.

### `Idgen`

An `Idgen` is a `Proxy`-based object that is both:
- **Callable** — calling it creates a new child `Idgen` with a further unique suffix.
- **Property-accessible** — accessing any property `wid.foo` returns a new `Idgen` for the child scope `"<parent>-foo"`, stably (same property always returns the same path string).

Converting an `Idgen` to a string (via `+ ""`, template literals, or `.toString()`) yields the actual ID string to use as an HTML `id` attribute.

```typescript
export interface Idgen {
  (pre?: string): Idgen;
  [key: string]: Idgen;
}
export function id(pre?: string): Idgen;
```

## Usage

```js
import { id } from "@hoge1e3/dom-id";

const wid = id("widget");

// Use as HTML id attributes
console.log(wid + "");          // e.g. "widget-3q"
console.log(wid.title + "");    // e.g. "widget-3q-title"
console.log(wid.list + "");     // e.g. "widget-3q-list"
console.log(wid.list.item + ""); // e.g. "widget-3q-list-item"
```

## Use with `@hoge1e3/component`

`Idgen` instances are passed into components as their stable identity. The `c()` function from `@hoge1e3/component` uses the string form of an `Idgen` as the element's `id`, so it can find and patch the element in the DOM on re-renders.

```js
import { id } from "@hoge1e3/dom-id";
import { c } from "@hoge1e3/component";
import { t } from "@hoge1e3/dom";
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";

const wid = id("app");
const count = ref(0);

// wid.counter is passed as the component's ID
const el = c(wid.counter, count, (n) => t.span(`Count: ${n}`));
document.body.appendChild(el);
```

## Notes

- IDs are unique per page load (counter-based), not persistent across reloads.
- The same property path always resolves to the same ID string for a given `Idgen` root, making IDs stable within a render tree.
- Internally uses `document.getElementById` to verify uniqueness when generating root IDs.
